x-enumeration-literals.name: Full Repayment Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The repayment amount is applied to fully settle the outstanding loan balance.

Comment:
Example:
A borrower pays the entire remaining loan amount and closes the loan account.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Partial Repayment Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Only part of the outstanding loan balance is applied, and the loan remains active.

Comment:
Example:
A borrower pays €500 toward a €10,000 loan balance.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Installment Repayment Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayment is applied to cover a scheduled installment according to the repayment schedule.

Comment:
Example:
A monthly mortgage payment applied to the current installment.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Advance Repayment Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayment is applied before the scheduled due date, reducing future obligations.

Comment:
Example:
A borrower pays the next months loan installment in advance.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Overdue Repayment Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayment is applied to clear overdue or past-due amounts first.

Comment:
Example:
A payment used to settle a missed loan installment from the previous month.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Component-Specific Repayment Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayment is applied to a specific loan component such as principal, interest, fees, or penalties.

Comment:
Example:
A borrower pays only outstanding interest.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Prepayment Repayment Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayment is applied toward reducing the principal earlier than scheduled, often shortening the loan term.

Comment:
Example:
A borrower makes a lump-sum payment to reduce the remaining loan balance.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Settlement Repayment Application
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayment is applied to fully settle the loan including principal, interest, fees, and penalties, leading to loan closure.

Comment:
Example:
A borrower pays all outstanding amounts to terminate the loan contract.
